nodes.x: Truly my soul waiteth upon God: from him cometh my salvation.
My soul fainteth for thy salvation: but I hope in thy word.
My soul, wait thou only upon God; for my expectation is from him.
And my soul shall be joyful in the Lord: it shall rejoice in his salvation.
I wait for the Lord, my soul doth wait, and in his word do I hope.
¶ And it shall be said in that day, Lo, this is our God; we have waited for him, and he will save us: this is the Lord; we have waited for him, we will be glad and rejoice in his salvation.
He only is my rock and my salvation; he is my defence; I shall not be greatly moved.
He only is my rock and my salvation: he is my defence; I shall not be moved.
But the Lord is my defence; and my God is the rock of my refuge.
The God of my rock; in him will I trust: he is my shield, and the horn of my salvation, my high tower, and my refuge, my saviour; thou savest me from violence.
He shall cry unto me, Thou art my father, my God, and the rock of my salvation.
The Lord is my rock, and my fortress, and my deliverer; my God, my strength, in whom I will trust; my buckler, and the horn of my salvation, and my high tower.
How long will ye imagine mischief against a man? ye shall be slain all of you: as a bowing wall shall ye be, and as a tottering fence.

And when ye shall see these sayings coming forth among you, then ye need not any longer spurn at the doings of the Lord, for the sword of his justice is in his right hand; and behold, at that day, if ye shall spurn at his doings he will cause that it shall soon overtake you.

O ye wicked and ye perverse generation; ye hardened and ye stiffnecked people, how long will ye suppose that the Lord will suffer you? Yea, how long will ye suffer yourselves to be led by foolish and blind guides? Yea, how long will ye choose darkness rather than light?

For I know that after my death ye will utterly corrupt yourselves, and turn aside from the way which I have commanded you; and evil will befall you in the latter days; because ye will do evil in the sight of the Lord, to provoke him to anger through the work of your hands.

For ye have acquired it all in unrighteousness,
    And ye shall be given over to a great curse.
And it shall come to pass, that as ye were a curse among the heathen, O house of Judah, and house of Israel; so will I save you, and ye shall be a blessing: fear not, but let your hands be strong.
They only consult to cast him down from his excellency: they delight in lies: they bless with their mouth, but they curse inwardly. Selah.
He that hateth dissembleth with his lips, and layeth up deceit within him;

But the Saint of God can see in these things the fulfilment of the words of God, ‘through his Prophets and Apostles — “ I nlso will choose their delusions, and will bring their fears upon them, because, when I called, none did answer ; when I spake, they did not hear ; but they did evil before mine eyes, and chose that in ’ which I delighted not.” (Isaiah lxvi. 4.) 4t And for this cause shall God…
Nevertheless they did flatter him with their mouth, and they lied unto him with their tongues.
He that blesseth his friend with a loud voice, rising early in the morning, it shall be counted a curse to him.
My soul, wait thou only upon God; for my expectation is from him.
Truly my soul waiteth upon God: from him cometh my salvation.
And the Spirit said unto me: Behold, what desirest thou?

And also my soul delighteth in the covenants of the Lord which he hath made to our fathers; yea, my soul delighteth in his grace, and in his justice, and power, and mercy in the great and eternal plan of deliverance from death.

The Spirit of the Lord God is upon me; because the Lord hath anointed me to preach good tidings unto the meek; he hath sent me to bind up the brokenhearted, to proclaim liberty to the captives, and the opening of the prison to them that are bound;
He only is my rock and my salvation: he is my defence; I shall not be moved.
He only is my rock and my salvation; he is my defence; I shall not be greatly moved.
In God is my salvation and my glory: the rock of my strength, and my refuge, is in God.
In God is my salvation and my glory: the rock of my strength, and my refuge, is in God.
The Lord is my strength and song, and is become my salvation.
The Lord liveth; and blessed be my rock; and exalted be the God of the rock of my salvation.
The Lord is my strength and song, and he is become my salvation: he is my God, and I will prepare him an habitation; my father’s God, and I will exalt him.
Trust in him at all times; ye people, pour out your heart before him: God is a refuge for us. Selah.
In God have I put my trust: I will not be afraid what man can do unto me.
So shall I have wherewith to answer him that reproacheth me: for I trust in thy word.
Though he slay me, yet will I trust in him: but I will maintain mine own ways before him.
He that trusteth in his own heart is a fool: but whoso walketh wisely, he shall be delivered.
Surely men of low degree are vanity, and men of high degree are a lie: to be laid in the balance, they are altogether lighter than vanity.
¶ Seeing there be many things that increase vanity, what is man the better?
Behold, they are all vanity; their works are nothing: their molten images are wind and confusion.
The desire of a man is his kindness: and a poor man is better than a liar.
The Lord knoweth the thoughts of man, that they are vanity.
Trust not in oppression, and become not vain in robbery: if riches increase, set not your heart upon them.
Seek not after riches nor the vain things of this world; for behold, you cannot carry them with you.
They that trust in their wealth, and boast themselves in the multitude of their riches;

True wealth consists in the skill to produce conveniences and comforts
from the elements. All the power and dignity that wealth can bestow
is a mere shadow, the substance is found in the bone and sinew of the
toiling millions. Well directed labor is the true power that supplies
our wants. It gives regal grandeur to potentates, education and
supplies to religious and political ministers, and…
Your riches are corrupted, and your garments are motheaten.
And behold, the time cometh that he curseth your riches, that they become slippery, that ye cannot hold them; and in the days of your poverty ye cannot retain them.
God hath spoken once; twice have I heard this; that power belongeth unto God.
And Christ hath said: If ye will have faith in me ye shall have power to do whatsoever thing is expedient in me.
And it shall be given unto you, power over that spirit; and you shall proclaim against that spirit with a loud voice that it is not of God—
And now behold, can ye dispute the power of God? For behold, doth not my voice shake the earth? And can ye not also behold me before you? And I am sent from God.
But verily, verily, I say unto you, that as many as receive me, to them will I give power to become the sons of God, even to them that believe on my name. Amen.
How is it that ye have not hearkened unto the word of the Lord?
Also unto thee, O Lord, belongeth mercy: for thou renderest to every man according to his work.
For the work of a man shall he render unto him, and cause every man to find according to his ways.
Let thy mercy, O Lord, be upon us, according as we hope in thee.
For thou, Lord, art good, and ready to forgive; and plenteous in mercy unto all them that call upon thee.
Thy mercy, O Lord, is in the heavens; and thy faithfulness reacheth unto the clouds.
And now thou beholdest, O Lord, that thy servants have done according to thy commandment.
